title: rapanea buxifolia
intro: Rapanea buxifolia, also known as the box-leaved sapindus, is a plant species native to the Pacific Islands, specifically in the islands of New Caledonia and Vanuatu. It is a shrub or small tree that has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Rapanea buxifolia include its ability to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its antibacterial and antifungal properties. In traditional herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of Rapanea buxifolia can be used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are believed to provide relief from these conditions.
